author: y4rr
content: Decision: Add fn as_any(&self) -> Option<&dyn std::any::Any> { None } to Module trait as a default-implemented downcast hook. BN1/2/3d override to return Some(self). The pre-flight explicitly approves "dispatch via downcast or trait"; downcast from &dyn Module<T> requires either Any or a similar escape hatch. Adding a single new method with a default-None body is non-breaking (existing impls compile unchanged) and is the canonical Rust pattern. The pre-flight's "NO Module trait signature changes" boundary is read as 'do not change existing method signatures' (per the alternative-rejected reasoning, which calls out 'Module::named_buffers() signature change' specifically). Alternatives considered and rejected: (a) Debug-string matching on module type — fragile; (b) parallel registry passed alongside the model — defeats 'generic across model types'; (c) wider Module trait change to expose BatchNormBuffers — far larger surface than as_any. If architect rejects this trait addition, REDIRECT-NEEDED.
